dropzone.formatHint: Click or drag to select audio files. All decryption is done locally.
dropzone.tip: Tip: Encrypted formats like NCM / KWM / KGM / QMC can be decrypted and converted to MP3, FLAC, and other standard formats right here. This app works entirely offline — all processing is done locally, no files are ever uploaded.
error.noOutputDir: Please select an output directory first
tooltip.bitrate: Bitrate controls audio quality. Higher bitrate (e.g. 320k) means better quality but larger files. 128k is acceptable for casual listening, 320k for audiophiles.
tooltip.vbr: Variable Bitrate allocates more data to complex passages and less to simple ones. Produces better quality-to-size ratio than CBR. VBR 0 is highest quality, VBR 9 is smallest file.
tooltip.sampleRate: Sample rate is the number of audio samples per second. 44100 Hz (CD quality) is standard for music. 48000 Hz is used for video. Higher rates preserve more high frequencies but increase file size.
tooltip.bitDepth: Bit depth determines dynamic range. 16-bit (CD quality) is sufficient for most music. 24-bit is used in studio recording for extra headroom. 32-bit float is for professional production.
tooltip.compression: Compression level controls the tradeoff between encode speed and file size in lossless formats (FLAC, ALAC). Level 0 encodes fastest but produces larger files. Levels 5–8 offer the best size/speed balance. Level 12 yields the smallest files but takes longest to encode.
tooltip.concurrentLimit: Controls how many files are converted simultaneously. Higher values process batches faster but consume more CPU and memory. Set to 1 for sequential file-by-file conversion. Enable “Auto-Adjust” (recommended) for best results based on your CPU core count.
tooltip.duplicateAction: Choose what happens when the output file already exists: Overwrite replaces the original (the old file will be lost); Skip leaves it untouched; Rename appends a number to the filename automatically (e.g. song_01.mp3).
tooltip.keyManagement: Some encrypted music formats require additional keys for decryption. QMCv2 uses an ekey string (obtained from the QQ Music client); KGG keys are extracted from the KuGou app database (kuwo.db or kgmusic.db). If decryption fails, try importing the relevant keys.
formatDesc.mp3: MP3 (MPEG-1 Audio Layer 3) — the most widely supported lossy audio format. Uses a psychoacoustic model (perceptual coding) with Joint Stereo and Bit Reservoir techniques to significantly reduce file size with negligible quality loss. The reference encoder is LAME, supporting CBR/VBR/ABR modes. Universally compatible across virtually all hardware devices and software platforms. Parameters: bitrate (128k–320k), sample rate, VBR/CBR.
formatDesc.flac: FLAC (Free Lossless Audio Codec) — open-source lossless compression with ~40%-60% size reduction, preserving the original PCM data bit-perfectly. Uses linear prediction with Rice coding, supports 4–32 bit depth up to 192 kHz sample rate. Built-in MD5 checksum for integrity verification, seektable for instant seeking. The preferred archival format for music collectors, widely supported across players and car systems. Parameters: compression level (0–12), sample rate, bit depth.
formatDesc.wav: WAV (Waveform Audio Format) — uncompressed PCM audio using the RIFF container, offering maximum compatibility. Stores raw audio sample values directly with zero compression, resulting in the largest file size with absolutely zero quality loss. Supports 8/16/24/32-bit depth and multi-channel (mono to 7.1). Ideal as an intermediate format for audio editing and mastering, also suited for scenarios demanding maximum compatibility. Parameters: sample rate, bit depth.
formatDesc.ogg: OGG (Vorbis) — open-source, patent-free lossy audio format. Ogg is the container layer, Vorbis the core codec. Uses quality-based encoding (-1 to 10) with channel coupling for improved stereo separation. Generally achieves better perceived quality than MP3 at the same bitrate. Free from patent licensing, widely adopted in game audio and open-source ecosystems. Parameters: bitrate, sample rate, VBR.
formatDesc.m4a: M4A (MPEG-4 Audio) — the audio-only extension of the MPEG-4 container (technically the same format as .mp4 video, designated as pure-audio). Typically contains AAC (lossy) or ALAC (lossless) streams. Inherits AAC’s excellent quality performance, natively supported in the Apple ecosystem (iTunes, iPhone, Apple Music). Supports chapter markers and embedded cover art. Parameters: bitrate, sample rate.
formatDesc.aac: AAC (Advanced Audio Coding) — modern lossy codec designed as the official successor to MP3 (MPEG-2/4 standards). Uses advanced coding tools including MDCT, Temporal Noise Shaping (TNS), and Spectral Band Replication (SBR). Delivers audibly better quality than MP3 at equivalent bitrates. HE-AAC (AAC+) further optimizes low-bitrate performance down to ~32 kbps. The standard codec for YouTube, streaming services, and digital radio. Parameters: bitrate (32k–512k), sample rate.
formatDesc.aiff: AIFF (Audio Interchange File Format) — Apple’s uncompressed audio format based on the IFF container, functionally similar to WAV but using big-endian byte order. Stores raw PCM sample data directly for zero quality loss with large file sizes. Supports the AIFF-C compressed variant (using MACE or other codecs). Widely used in professional audio production (Logic Pro, Pro Tools) and the macOS ecosystem. Parameters: sample rate, bit depth.
formatDesc.alac: ALAC (Apple Lossless Audio Codec) — Apple’s lossless compression format, achieving ~40%-60% size reduction while preserving bit-perfect original quality. Uses frame-based compression with highly efficient decoding (lower CPU overhead than FLAC). Open-sourced by Apple in 2011. Deeply integrated with the M4A container, seamless in the Apple ecosystem (iTunes, Apple Music, iPhone). The archival lossless choice for Apple users. Parameters: sample rate, bit depth.
formatDesc.opus: Opus — modern open-source lossy codec (IETF RFC 6716) with a hybrid SILK + CELT architecture. Covers 6 kbps speech to 510 kbps high-fidelity music across full bandwidth (narrowband to 20 kHz full-range). Delivers superior quality over MP3 and AAC at equivalent bitrates, with ultra-low latency (as low as 5 ms) ideal for real-time communication and streaming. Royalty-free, natively supported by all major browsers and modern players. Parameters: bitrate (6k–510k), sample rate, VBR.

loudness.title: Loudness Normalization
loudness.targetHint: -14 LUFS is the standard for streaming platforms (Spotify, Apple Music, YouTube). Higher LUFS values (closer to -6) produce louder output; lower values (closer to -23) produce quieter output.
